syncStatus: local main is in sync with origin/main — nothing to push
summary: LINE-integrated delivery ordering system for Love Pier Beach Cafe: a guided full-screen order wizard (LINE login -> GPS distance check -> menu -> summary -> PromptPay QR -> success) plus an Events feature (list, detail page, gallery) and an admin backoffice for orders/settings/events.
note: This session (2026-07-22, continued) hardened the live payment path. Three things landed + verified against REAL production payments: (1) the SlipOK code-1014 Ref.1 fallback was silently disabled in prod because it was gated on NEXT_PUBLIC_PROMPTPAY_REF, which Next.js inlines at BUILD time — a build predating that Vercel env var baked it as undefined, so every genuine Bill Payment slip failed 1014. Now defaults to the shop's fixed Ref.1/biller id so it works regardless of build-time env (commit 5a83cb9). (2) Vercel build broke again with 'No Next.js version detected' — Vercel CLI 56.x now resolves the next VERSION from installed node_modules, not just the package.json dependency string, so installCommand 'echo skip' left nothing to detect; changed it to 'npm install' at root (commit b50f926). (3) LINE_MESSAGING_TOKEN was finally set in Vercel — the payment-confirmation Flex card now actually reaches the LINE OA (verified live on order LP260722-4082: both the order-received card AND the '✅ ชำระเงินสำเร็จ' card arrived). REMAINING: SlipOK still returns 1014 for Mae Manee Bill Payment even after the shop linked the แม่มณี account to branch 71639 — because the slip's receiver biller id comes back MASKED ('XXXXXXXXXXX1402') so SlipOK can't match it to the registered full '010554511741402'. This is a SlipOK-side limitation; shop must contact SlipOK support. It does NOT affect taking payment (our fallback marks paid) — only SlipOK's own dashboard tally + SlipOK's cross-slip duplicate detection. >>> RESOLVED LATER THE SAME DAY (see the SlipOK featureArea): SlipOK now accepts the masked Mae Manee biller and returns success for genuine shop payments.
noteLatest: Later on 2026-07-22 (same day, continued): (1) SlipOK 1014 is RESOLVED — re-tested real stored slips through the live SlipOK API and genuine shop payments (receiver BILLERID ...1402, ref1 REF001) now return success:true natively, so they log in the SlipOK dashboard AND SlipOK's cross-slip duplicate detection now covers Bill Payment slips. Our 1014 Ref.1 fallback is now just a safety net. The 3 pending orders that still return 1014 are NOT shop payments at all — inspecting receiver revealed LP260717-2240 (฿735) was paid to METROPOLITAN WATERWORKS (a water bill!), and LP260720-7453 (฿20) + LP260721-2094 (฿550) went to personal e-wallets (...8206, ...6002). SlipOK is correctly rejecting bogus slips; do NOT mark them paid. (2) Fixed event images not rendering in /admin/events — the list thumbnail was the only admin <Image> missing `unoptimized` and next.config had no images.remotePatterns for Supabase (commit 8b3ef50). (3) Recolored the LINE Flex cards (commit 4c91891) and the public order-status page (commit 944910c) from the old green #2d6a1f to the site's warm palette, and stripped the emoji icons off the order-status card.

featureAreas.status: done — fixed a live payment-blocking bug this session
featureAreas.notes: The QR was missing Ref.1 entirely, then (once added) used a per-order dynamic Ref.1 — both rejected by the bank ('QR code ไม่ถูกต้อง' / 'เลขที่อ้างอิงไม่ถูกต้อง'). Root cause found by decoding the shop's real physical Mae Manee QR sticker with a QR reader: this SCB Bill Payment biller (010554511741402) requires exact FIXED values — Ref.1='REF001', Ref.2='0', and a Terminal Label (EMVCo tag 62 sub-tag 07)='SKD68W744913' — which the code never sent at all. billPaymentPayload() now accepts terminalLabel; verified byte-for-byte identical to the real sticker's payload (CRC included), then confirmed with a real K PLUS bank payment. New env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_PROMPTPAY_REF=REF001, NEXT_PUBLIC_PROMPTPAY_REF2=0, NEXT_PUBLIC_PROMPTPAY_TERMINAL_LABEL=SKD68W744913 (set in Vercel, not just .env.local — NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars are baked at build time).

featureAreas.status: done — SlipOK now accepts the Mae Manee Bill Payment biller; 1014 fully resolved (verified live 2026-07-22)
featureAreas.notes: History: (1) the API key kept changing when viewed in the dashboard (regeneration); working key captured (SLIPOKL4KI95U, len 13, branch 71639). (2) SlipOK's log:true 'main account' check doesn't recognize the Mae Manee Bill Payment receiver (shared account) and returns code 1014 for genuine payments; added a Ref.1 fallback. (3) THIS session: that fallback was silently DEAD in production. It was gated on `const BILLER_REF1 = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PROMPTPAY_REF || ''` — NEXT_PUBLIC_* is inlined at build time, so a deploy built before the var was set in Vercel baked it as undefined, making the `&& BILLER_REF1` guard false and disabling the whole fallback. Fixed (commit 5a83cb9): default to the shop's fixed values `BILLER_REF1='REF001'` and `BILLER_ID='010554511741402'` (both printed on the physical QR sticker, not secret), and match on Ref.1 OR receiver biller-id (receiver.proxy.value comes back MASKED like 'XXXXXXXXXXX1402' so we compare trailing visible digits via BILLER_ID.endsWith). Verified by replaying real stored slips through the actual verifySlip() with env present AND env cleared — both return verified:true. IMPORTANT UNRESOLVED: SlipOK's OWN API still returns 1014 even after the shop linked the แม่มณี account (010554511741402) to branch 71639 with the toggle on — confirmed by re-testing a FRESH slip (LP260722-4082) live: still code 1014. Root cause is the masked biller id; SlipOK can't be configured around it from the dashboard. Shop must ask SlipOK support to accept biller 010554511741402 for branch 71639. Consequence: SlipOK never logs these transactions, so (a) the amount never shows in the SlipOK dashboard and (b) SlipOK's cross-slip duplicate detection does NOT cover Bill Payment slips — a customer could reuse one slip across multiple orders. Our only current dup guard is 'this order is already paid'. >>> RESOLVED 2026-07-22 (later same day): replayed real stored slips through the live SlipOK API — genuine shop payments (LP260722-4082, LP260721-4425; receiver proxy BILLERID 'XXXXXXXXXXX1402', ref1 'REF001') now return success:true from SlipOK itself, so verifySlip() hits the primary success branch and never needs the 1014 fallback. Consequences reversed: SlipOK DOES log these now, so the dashboard tally works and SlipOK's own cross-slip duplicate detection covers Bill Payment slips.

The '✅ ชำระเงินสำเร็จ' Flex card (buildPaymentConfirmedFlex, pushed via pushToUser in pages/api/verify-slip.js) requires LINE_MESSAGING_TOKEN. It was previously unset in Vercel, so pushToUser returned {skipped:true} silently and no card reached the LINE OA even though the order stored a valid lineUserId. Shop set the token in Vercel this session; verified on order LP260722-4082 that both the order-received card and the payment-confirmed card now arrive in the @lovepier.cafe chat. NB for future debugging: pushToUser also needs the LIFF's LINE Login channel and the OA's Messaging API channel to belong to the same OA/provider, or the userId won't be pushable.

buildPaymentConfirmedFlex() — sent via pages/api/verify-slip.js the moment SlipOK verifies a slip, alongside SlipOK's own reply card in the same LINE chat. Styled in the shop's green brand tone (#2d6a1f, matching the existing order-received card) rather than a new color.

featureAreas.area: Brand colour of LINE cards + order status page
featureAreas.notes: The LINE Flex cards (lib/orderFlex.js) and the public order-status page (pages/order/[orderNo].js) were green (#2d6a1f), which matched nothing on the site. Both now use the real theme tokens from styles/globals.css: espresso #3a2818 for card headers and primary buttons, gold #c9a96e for the 'Love Pier Beach Cafe' subtitle (gold is only ever used on dark backgrounds — it fails contrast on white), deep brown #4a3520 for order numbers and totals on white, cream #f5f3ef page background. The order page's 'paid' badge became a SOLID espresso pill with cream text so it stays visually distinct from the pale amber 'pending' badge; the other status colours (pending amber / preparing blue / cancelled red) stay semantic on purpose. All emoji icons (✅ 📍 🛵 💰) were removed from the order-status card at the user's request — the address row gained a real 'ที่อยู่ :' label because the pin emoji had been its only label. Green #2d6a1f still remains in components/CartDrawer.js, components/delivery/OrderFlow.js and app/admin/orders/page.tsx — not yet rethemed.

Uploaded event images showed a broken-image icon in the admin list. Storage was fine all along (bucket public, files present, URLs return 200 image/webp with access-control-allow-origin *). Cause: app/admin/events/page.tsx rendered the thumbnail with next/image and was the ONLY admin <Image> missing `unoptimized` (menu-manager, promotions page and PromotionModal all had it), while next.config.mjs had no images.remotePatterns — so Next refused the external Supabase host. Fixed both ways: added `unoptimized` to the thumbnail and whitelisted https://*.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/** in remotePatterns. NB: the in-app preview browser cannot display ANY external-host image (localhost images load, Supabase ones do not) — that is a sandbox limitation and must not be mistaken for this bug; verify external images on a real device.
Was generating the 3 responsive widths (480/960/1440) sequentially — 3 stacked resize+upload round-trips per click. Now runs them in Promise.all (each width is independent), ~2-3x faster.

featureAreas.status: done — fixed twice; latest fix this session
featureAreas.notes: The real app lives in love-pier-main/, built via a custom buildCommand in vercel.json that cd's into it. Vercel's framework detection runs at the repo root and hard-fails with 'No Next.js version detected' before the custom build command. First fix (e8779ff) declared `next` in the root package.json and kept installCommand 'echo skip'. That stopped working: Vercel CLI 56.x now resolves the next VERSION from the installed node_modules (reading next/package.json), not just the dependency string, so 'echo skip' left nothing installed and detection failed again. Current fix (commit b50f926): installCommand = 'npm install --no-audit --no-fund' at root, whose only dependency is next@16.2.6 — a ~16-package / ~4s install (dry-run verified) that gives Vercel a resolvable node_modules/next. buildCommand still does the real install+build inside love-pier-main. outputDirectory stays love-pier-main/.next.

Short pages (e.g. /delivery's welcome step — just a hero + footer, no full-height wrapper) showed a jarring blank white gap below the footer on tall viewports, since html/body had no background set. Added background-color: var(--color-bg) to both — had to be added as two separate rules, since Turbopack's CSS build silently drops the html side when declared as a compound `html, body` selector alongside the project's existing standalone `html { scroll-behavior }` rule (a real build-tool quirk, not a CSS mistake).
pages/events/[id].js hero image changed from 4:3 to 3:4 to match the /events grid card poster ratio, per explicit user request for visual consistency.

featureAreas.status: current architecture — replaced the earlier DeliveryGate + CartDrawer bottom-sheet
featureAreas.notes: Single linear wizard on /delivery: welcome -> distance check -> menu -> summary -> payment -> success. Full-screen, app-like — site nav/footer are hidden during steps 2-6 via lib/chrome.js. Out-of-radius customers must tick an explicit acknowledgement before continuing. makePaymentRef() still generates a per-order reference for our own reconciliation (LINE message, order record) but it is NOT embedded in the QR anymore — the QR's Ref.1 must be the biller's fixed value (see PromptPay entry above).

pages/api/delivery-distance.js computes distance (haversine or Google Routes, chosen in /admin/settings) and delivery fee (lib/deliveryFee.js: base + per-km, server-side only, recomputed again in /api/orders — never trusts a client-sent fee).

lib/orderFlex.js builds a Tassana-style card sent both customer-side (liff.sendMessages) and shop-side (Messaging API push to the customer) via lib/lineMessaging.js. Links to a public /order/[orderNo] status page.

/events splits into Upcoming/Past sections derived from the date, with portrait cards, a location badge, and desaturated styling for past events. /events/[id] detail page: hero image, sticky info sidebar, category + price badges, description, photo gallery.

/admin/orders (list + status dropdown + pending badge in sidebar), /admin/settings (distance method, shop coords, radius, delivery fee, SlipOK keys), /admin/events (extended CRUD). Login: cafe.lovepier@gmail.com.
Both pages render components/menu/MenuExperience.js (single source of truth) so future menu-layout edits apply to both.

knownBugsFixedHistorically: GPS error text froze in the wrong language (stale-closure i18n bug) — fixed by storing error codes instead of pre-translated strings.
Leaflet crash 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading layerPointToLatLng)' — fixed by calling map.setView() before circle.getBounds().
CartDrawer/OrderFlow read deliverySession only once on app mount (before the gate finished) — fixed to re-check on relevant open/step events.
Event PUT route reset all fields (including gallery) on a partial update like drag-reorder — fixed to only touch fields present in the request.
PromptPay Bill Payment QR missing/wrong Ref.1, Ref.2, and Terminal Label — customers could not pay at all. Fixed by matching the biller's registered fixed values exactly (see PromptPay entry above).
SlipOK auto-verification rejected every real payment with 'บัญชีผู้รับไม่ตรงกับบัญชีหลักของร้าน' — fixed with a Ref.1 fallback match for Bill Payment receivers.
Vercel deploy hard-failed with 'No Next.js version detected' — fixed by declaring next in the root package.json.

Unanswered as of end of session — needs a reply before any code change.

followUps.item: [DIAGNOSED 2026-07-22] Pending orders whose slips fail SlipOK are BOGUS SLIPS, not a system fault
followUps.detail: Inspected each failing slip's actual receiver via the live SlipOK API: LP260717-2240 (฿735) was paid to 'METROPOLITAN WATERWORKS' biller ...6309 — the customer attached their WATER BILL slip; LP260720-7453 (฿20) went to a personal e-wallet (...8206, น.ส. วิลาสินี); LP260721-2094 (฿550) went to a personal e-wallet (...6002, น.ส. ณัฐชาริณีย์). None were paid to the shop's biller (...1402), so SlipOK's 1014 is CORRECT. Do NOT mark these paid — they are wrong or fraudulent slips. Leave pending / cancel.

followUps.item: [RESOLVED 2026-07-22] SlipOK returned 1014 for Mae Manee Bill Payment — no longer an issue
followUps.detail: Shop linked the แม่มณี account (010554511741402) to branch 71639 in the SlipOK dashboard (shows 'เชื่อมต่อแล้ว', toggle on, alongside the SCB acct 6402723755). Re-tested a FRESH live slip (LP260722-4082) through the real SlipOK API afterwards: STILL code 1014. Root cause is that the slip's receiver.proxy.value comes back MASKED ('XXXXXXXXXXX1402'), so SlipOK can't match the shared Mae Manee biller. Cannot be fixed from the dashboard. Shop was given a ready-to-send Thai support message asking SlipOK to accept biller 010554511741402 for branch 71639. Until then the SlipOK dashboard won't tally these and SlipOK's duplicate check won't cover them. Our own verify-slip fallback still marks orders paid correctly.
followUps.status: resolved — verified live: genuine shop payments now return success:true from SlipOK

followUps.item: Our own cross-order duplicate-slip guard — now largely unnecessary, but still a cheap belt-and-braces
followUps.detail: The original reason (SlipOK never logged Bill Payment slips, so its dedup didn't cover them) is gone as of 2026-07-22: SlipOK now logs and dedups these slips itself. A local guard in pages/api/verify-slip.js (reject if result.transRef already exists on another paid order) would still add defence-in-depth if SlipOK ever regresses. Low priority now.
